<current_datetime>2026-05-12T14:26:45.950Z</current_datetime>

I have this MSTest data-driven test using DynamicData with object arrays. Is there a more type-safe way to write this in modern MSTest?

```csharp
[TestMethod]
[DynamicData(nameof(TestData))]
public void Validate_ReturnsExpected(string input, bool expected)
{
    var result = Validator.IsValid(input);
    Assert.AreEqual(expected, result);
}

public static IEnumerable<object[]> TestData =>
[
    ["valid@email.com", true],
    ["invalid", false],
    ["", false],
];
```

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a more type-safe way to write DynamicData tests in modern MSTest. This is about MSTest v3/v4 features where you can use `ValueTuple` instead of `object[]` for DynamicData. Let me invoke the writing-mstest-tests skill since this is about MSTest API usage.

Actually, looking at the available skills, I don't see a "writing-mstest-tests" skill listed. But the question is straightforward - modern MSTest (v3+) supports `IEnumerable<(T1, T2, ...)>` (value tuples) for DynamicData, which is more type-safe than `object[]`.


I'll just provide a direct answer about this MSTest feature.
